summary: ai-pro-sdk decrypts and spawns hidden payload smuggled via data-blockv dependency
details: The package's dist/index.js contains an undocumented loader that imports a ciphertext token from the sibling dependency data-blockv, DES-decrypts it with a hardcoded password via crypto-js, writes the decrypted script to a temp file with mode 0o600, and spawns node on that file. The README advertises only a Vercel AI SDK provider for Claude and does not disclose this behavior. Because package.json main points at the loader file, importing ai-pro-sdk triggers execution of the decrypted payload; the two-package split (encrypted bytes shipped in data-blockv, key+decrypt+spawn shipped in ai-pro-sdk) is a smuggling channel that lets the attacker change the executed code by publishing a new data-blockv version without touching ai-pro-sdk.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
